generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow licensed realtors to log in using authenticated credentials (username and password) before accessing any application features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access must be restricted to authenticated users to protect sensitive client and property data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based authorization to ensure only licensed and authorized realtors can access the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authorization controls prevent unauthorized individuals from accessing confidential MLS and client information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to search MLS listings by criteria including location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms, and number of bathrooms.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS search is a core feature enabling realtors to find relevant properties for their clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display property images associated with each MLS listing, supporting multiple images per property.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual property information is essential for realtors to evaluate and present listings to clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an interactive map view for each property listing, showing the property's geographic location.
generated_requirements.rationale: Map views help realtors and clients understand property location and surrounding context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions from the realtor's current GPS location to a selected property listing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Driving directions support field realtors in navigating efficiently to property showings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report for a selected property, incorporating comparable listings and recent sales data.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA reports are a primary tool for realtors to support property valuations and client presentations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to export or share CMA reports in a portable format (e.g., PDF) for use in client presentations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable reports enable realtors to present professional valuation documents to clients in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to view, add, edit, and delete client contact records including name, phone number, email address, and notes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Client relationship management is a core use case requiring full CRUD operations on contact data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to initiate a phone call to a client directly from the client's contact record within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: In-app calling streamlines communication by eliminating the need to switch between applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to send appointment requests to clients, specifying date, time, location, and subject.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment scheduling is essential for coordinating property showings and client meetings in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to receive and view client responses (accept, decline, or propose new time) to appointment requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Receiving appointment responses ensures realtors can confirm or adjust scheduling without returning to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize contact information bidirectionally with the realtor's office system to ensure up-to-date client records on the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bidirectional sync ensures consistency between field and office data, preventing outdated contact information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize calendar and appointment data bidirectionally with the realtor's office system to maintain a unified schedule.
generated_requirements.rationale: Calendar synchronization prevents scheduling conflicts between field activities and office-managed appointments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in an offline mode, allowing realtors to access previously cached listings, contacts, and calendar data without a network connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline capability ensures continuous productivity when network connectivity is unavailable in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect network connectivity status and seamlessly transition between online and offline modes without requiring user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent mode switching ensures a seamless user experience regardless of connectivity conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue data changes made in offline mode and automatically synchronize them with the server upon restoration of network connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Queued sync ensures no data is lost when the realtor works offline and later reconnects.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be self-installing, allowing realtors to download and install the application on their smart device without requiring IT assistance or formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-installation reduces deployment friction and operational costs for real estate organizations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an intuitive user interface that enables a new realtor to complete core tasks (search, view listing, contact client) without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: An intuitive UI reduces onboarding time and supports adoption by realtors with varying technical skill levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS search results within 3 seconds under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response times are critical for maintaining realtor productivity during client interactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load property images for a listing within 5 seconds on a standard mobile data connection (4G/LTE or better).
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely image loading ensures realtors can present property visuals to clients without delays.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a CMA report within 10 seconds of the realtor's request under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prompt CMA generation supports real-time client consultations without disruptive wait times.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 simultaneous active users without degradation in response time or functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability to thousands of concurrent users is explicitly required to support large realtor networks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to accommodate growth in the number of users, listings, and data volume without requiring architectural redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: Horizontal scalability ensures the platform can grow with the business without costly re-engineering.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be compatible with and fully functional on iOS (version 15 and above) and Android (version 11 and above) mobile operating systems.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall comply with the corporate standards for user interface creation for internally and externally used applications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All screens created as part of the Disputes application must comply with corporate standards for interface creation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The list of dispute cases that are displayed after a search is performed must be color coded for easy identification of dispute cases based upon the dispute case status.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of the cardmember and merchant services representatives shall use the Disputes application regularly after a 2-day training course.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of cardmember services representatives shall be able to successfully create a dispute case on the first encounter after completing the training course.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of merchant services representatives shall be able to successfully perform a follow up action on a dispute case on the first encounter after completing the training course.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The maximum wait time for a user navigating from one screen to another within the Disputes application shall be no more than 5 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall support 350 concurrent users without any degradation of performance in the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall be available 24x7x365 with the exception of the following scheduled maintenance windows. Monday â€“ Saturday 3:00am to 4:00am EST Sunday 1:00amd to 5:00am EST
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall interface with the Statement Database. The Statement Database provides the transaction details to the Disputes System. All transaction details must be obtained from the Statement Database
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall interface with the Cardmember Information Database. The Cardmember Information Database provides detailed information with regard to a cardmember. All cardmember detail information must be obtained from the Cardmember Information Database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes applications shall interface with the Merchant Information Database. The Merchant Information Database provides detailed information with regard to the merchant. All merchant detail information shall be obtained from the Merchant Information Database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall interface with the Letters application. This will allow the Disputes application to request letters as part of the dispute initiation and dispute follow up process. All letter requests must be sent to the Print Letter Utility application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall interface with the Cardmember Posting and Billing System. This will allow the Disputes application to request adjustments to cardmember and merchant's accounts. All adjustment requests must be sent to the Cardmember Posting and Billing System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall ensure that only users with the appropriate access level are allowed to initiate dispute requests.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only users with Update access must be able to initiate dispute requests.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall ensure that only authorized users are allowed to logon to the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All authorized users once authenticated must be able to logon to the Disputes System
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall generate a User Report that details any new users that have been added and any changes to an existing user's access level. The report will be reviewed for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All additions of new users and modifications to user's access shall be recorded on the User Report.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If a user account is revoked it can only be re-instantiated by the System Administrator.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only the System Administrator shall be able to reset a revoked user logon account
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall manipulate and view business data. Users will not have direct access to data files or databases.

ground_truth_requirements.text: All updates to data files or database must be initiated from the Disputes System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application must conform to the legal requirements as specified by the Merchant Operating Regulations.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All business rules specified in the Disputes System shall be in compliance with the Merchant Operating Regulations.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application must conform to the legal requirements as specified by Regulation E and Regulation Z that govern credit card disputes processing.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All business rules specified in the Disputes System shall be in compliance to the guidelines of Regulation E and Regulation Z.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application must maintain a detailed history of every action that a user takes on a dispute case. This ensures a complete audit trail if questions arise later on with regard to a particular dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All actions that modify an existing dispute case must be recorded in the case history.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must be accessible by both internal and external users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent users from accessing any dispute cases that do not belong to their cardholder base.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide different levels of access with regard to disputes case initiation and follow-up actions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide update access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the users to select disputable transactions (based on the age of the transaction) from a user interface and initiate a dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) on the selected transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the user the ability to initiate a single dispute case on multiple transactions that belong to a single merchant.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the user to create three unique types of ticket retrieval requests. The three types of ticket retrieval requests are (1) Request for original receipt (2) Request for a copy of the receipt or (3) Request for a portfolio. A portfolio consists of documentation that would provide proof of a purchase such as the documentation that is received from a car rental agency that is more than a sales receipt.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent external users from requesting original receipts. Requests for original receipts are restricted to internal users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of ticket retrieval request that contains the following information; the dispute case number the type of retrieval requested (copy original or portfolio) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall allow the user to create or initiate a chargeback request. The system must prompt the user to enter the required information to initiate a chargeback request. The chargeback request results in a printed chargeback notification that is sent to merchant indicating that their account will be charged back if no proof is provided that the disputed transaction is valid.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request. The confirmation must contain the following information; the dispute case number the type of chargeback requested (pending or immediate) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100% of the time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction. The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. The search method must include the ability to search by (1) the dispute case number (2) the merchant account number (3) the cardmember account number and (4) the issuer number. In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) the case status (open closed or all) and the dispute reason code.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color -coding the line item in the list. For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For any systematic (non-user initiated) action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated. Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.rationale: Modular design reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements, such as adding new external data feed integrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include automated unit and integration test suites with a minimum code coverage of 80%.
generated_requirements.rationale: High test coverage enables confident refactoring and reduces regression risk during ongoing maintenance.

generated_requirements.text: All configuration parameters, including tenant settings, preferred supplier lists, and role permissions, shall be manageable without requiring a code deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalizing configuration reduces operational overhead and allows rapid adjustments to business rules without developer involvement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on both Windows Server and Linux-based server environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-platform server support accommodates the diverse infrastructure of multiple insurance company clients.

generated_requirements.text: The client-facing application shall function correctly on the latest two major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari browsers without requiring browser plugins.
generated_requirements.rationale: Broad browser compatibility ensures usability across the varied device and browser environments found in offices and repair shops.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a responsive user interface that is fully functional on tablet devices with screen sizes of 10 inches or larger.
generated_requirements.rationale: Repair shop estimators may use tablets on the shop floor, requiring the interface to adapt to smaller touch-based screens.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce tenant-level data isolation at the database layer, ensuring queries cannot return data belonging to a different insurance company tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Database-level isolation provides a technical guarantee of data separation that cannot be bypassed by application-layer misconfiguration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a security audit log of all authentication events, role changes, and data export actions, retained for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term security logs support forensic investigation, compliance audits, and detection of insider threats.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver requested audit reports within 30 seconds for data sets covering up to 12 months of claims history.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely report generation is necessary to support adjuster and supervisory workflows without introducing unacceptable wait times.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to accommodate a 3x increase in user load without requiring architectural changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Horizontal scalability supports business growth as additional insurance companies are onboarded as tenants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a confirmation dialog and require explicit user confirmation before permanently deleting any claim, estimate, or facility record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Confirmation prompts prevent accidental deletion of critical records that may be difficult or impossible to recover.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to view historical facility ratings and associated audit reports for trend analysis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Historical trend visibility enables supervisors to make data-driven decisions about facility assignments over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard for adjusters and supervisory adjusters displaying key metrics such as open claims, pending estimate reviews, average recycled parts usage rate, and facility ratings.
generated_requirements.rationale: A summary dashboard improves situational awareness and allows supervisors to quickly identify claims or facilities requiring attention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data inputs on both the client and server sides, rejecting malformed or out-of-range values and displaying descriptive error messages to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dual-layer input validation prevents corrupt data from entering the system and protects against injection-based security attacks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to search for available conference rooms by date, time range, building, floor, capacity, and available equipment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core search functionality is essential for employees to find suitable rooms efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to reserve a conference room for a specified date, time, and duration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Room reservation is the primary functional purpose of the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to create a meeting, define an agenda, and associate it with a reserved conference room.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agenda management supports structured meeting preparation for organizers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to invite attendees to a meeting by selecting from an employee directory or entering email addresses.
generated_requirements.rationale: Invitation management is a core collaboration feature for meeting organizers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send meeting invitations to attendees via email upon scheduling or updating a meeting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email-based invitations ensure attendees are notified through a standard communication channel.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow attendees to accept, decline, or propose a new time for a meeting invitation from within the system or via email response.
generated_requirements.rationale: RSVP functionality enables organizers to track attendance and adjust plans accordingly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated reminders to meeting organizers and accepted attendees prior to a scheduled meeting, with configurable reminder lead times.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reminders reduce no-shows and help participants prepare for upcoming meetings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send real-time notifications to the meeting organizer when attendees respond to invitations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Organizers need timely awareness of attendee responses to manage meeting logistics.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an interactive building map showing the location of conference rooms, including room availability status, on each floor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual maps help employees quickly locate and identify available rooms within a building.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically update room availability schedules in real time when a reservation is created, modified, or cancelled.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time schedule updates prevent double-booking and ensure data accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to cancel an existing room reservation, automatically releasing the room and notifying all invited attendees.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cancellation with notification ensures rooms are freed promptly and attendees are informed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to modify an existing reservation, including changing the room, date, time, duration, or attendee list.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modification support accommodates changing meeting requirements without requiring full re-booking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to reserve equipment (e.g., projectors, video conferencing units, whiteboards) in association with a room reservation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Equipment reservation ensures necessary tools are available and not double-booked.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support equipment transport requests, allowing employees to request that equipment be moved to a specified room by a specified time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transport request tracking ensures logistical support staff are informed of equipment movement needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track the current reservation status and location of all managed equipment items.
generated_requirements.rationale: Equipment tracking prevents conflicts and supports efficient resource utilization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with the ability to generate reservation reports filtered by date range, building, room, organizer, or department.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting gives managers visibility into resource utilization for planning and accountability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with multiple calendar views (daily, weekly, monthly) of meeting schedules across one or more buildings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple views allow managers to monitor scheduling patterns at different levels of granularity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, distinguishing at minimum between Employee, Meeting Organizer, Manager, and System Administrator roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access ensures users can only perform actions appropriate to their responsibilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a System Administrator to configure buildings, floors, rooms, room capacities, room attributes, and available equipment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrative configuration supports deployment across diverse office environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a System Administrator to configure system-wide settings including reminder lead times, notification templates, working hours, and time zones.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable settings allow the system to be tailored to each organization's operational policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support branding customization, allowing administrators to configure the organization's logo, color scheme, and application name displayed in the UI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Branding support allows the system to align with the customer organization's visual identity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with standard SMTP-compatible email servers to send and receive meeting invitations, responses, and notifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email server integration is required for all invitation and notification workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with standard relational database management systems (e.g., Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, PostgreSQL) for persistent data storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: RDBMS integration ensures compatibility with enterprise database infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multiple human languages in the user interface, including at minimum English, and shall be architected to allow addition of further language packs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multilingual support is required for global organizations with non-English-speaking employees.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multiple time zones, displaying dates and times in the local time zone of the authenticated user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Time zone support is essential for accurate scheduling across geographically distributed offices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent double-booking of a conference room by rejecting or warning on any reservation request that conflicts with an existing confirmed reservation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict detection is fundamental to the integrity of the reservation system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to view their own upcoming and past reservations and meeting invitations in a personal dashboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: A personal dashboard gives users a consolidated view of their scheduling activity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support recurring meeting reservations (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) with the ability to edit a single occurrence or all future occurrences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Recurring reservations reduce manual effort for regularly scheduled meetings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate users against the organization's existing identity provider (e.g., LDAP or Active Directory) using standard protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise identity integration eliminates the need for separate credential management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an audit log of all reservation creation, modification, and cancellation events, recording the acting user, timestamp, and changed data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs support accountability, troubleshooting, and compliance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated search or page navigation action within 3 seconds under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast response times are critical for user productivity in a frequently used enterprise tool.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 500 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response time beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large organizations require the system to handle high concurrency without performance issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and confirm a room reservation transaction within 2 seconds under peak load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reservation transactions must be fast to prevent user frustration and reduce race conditions.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the browser client and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive meeting and personnel data from interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session timeout after a configurable period of inactivity, requiring re-authentication before further access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access from unattended browser sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall protect against common web application vulnerabilities as defined by the OWASP Top 10, including SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to OWASP Top 10 mitigations is a baseline security requirement for enterprise web applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to administrative configuration functions exclusively to users assigned the System Administrator role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting administrative access prevents unauthorized changes to system configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall be accessible via any modern standards-compliant web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) without requiring browser plugins or extensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser independence ensures all employees can access the system regardless of their workstation configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be implemented in Java and deployable on standard Java EE/Jakarta EE-compatible application servers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Java-based implementation ensures portability across operating systems and enterprise server environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to support users with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance ensures the system is usable by employees with visual or motor impairments.

generated_requirements.text: A new employee with no prior training shall be able to search for a room and complete a basic reservation within 10 minutes of first use.
generated_requirements.rationale: High learnability minimizes training overhead and accelerates adoption across the organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, actionable error messages in the user's selected language whenever a user action fails (e.g., room conflict, invalid input).
generated_requirements.rationale: Informative error messages reduce user confusion and support self-service error recovery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum uptime of 99.5% measured on a monthly basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is required for an enterprise scheduling system that employees depend on daily.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned failure and restore full service within 30 minutes (RTO), with no more than 1 hour of data loss (RPO).
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RTO and RPO targets ensure business continuity in the event of system failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of all reservation, configuration, and user data to a separate storage location.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups protect against data loss due to hardware failure or corruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a documented layered architecture with separation of presentation, business logic, and data access layers to facilitate long-term maintainability.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-structured architecture reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements and bug fixes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide comprehensive administrator and developer documentation, including deployment guides, configuration references, and API documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Complete documentation reduces administrative overhead and supports the multi-year maintenance lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support addition of new language packs without requiring changes to application source code or redeployment of the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalized language resources allow localization updates with minimal operational disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configuration of multiple office buildings and their associated floors and rooms within a single system instance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-building support within one instance reduces infrastructure costs for large organizations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all application errors and warnings to a centralized, configurable logging facility with sufficient detail to diagnose and resolve issues without access to production user data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralized diagnostic logging reduces mean time to resolution for production incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow inventory managers to create, read, update, and delete product formulas, specifying ingredients and their quantities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core formula management is the primary responsibility of inventory managers and drives all downstream calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the definition of semi-products within a product formula, allowing a semi-product to itself reference a sub-formula of ingredients.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical formula structures are required to accurately represent complex recipes that use pre-prepared components.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow formula administrators to define one or more substitutionary ingredients for any ingredient within a product formula, along with substitution ratios.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ingredient substitutions must be captured in formulas so the system can account for them during inventory adjustment calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically select a substitutionary ingredient when the primary ingredient's current inventory level is insufficient to fulfill the calculated demand.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic substitution logic ensures inventory adjustment documents reflect real-world ingredient usage accurately.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate an Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) document at the end of each business day based on recorded sales data and the applicable product formulas.
generated_requirements.rationale: Daily automated IQA generation is the central workflow that links sales activity to inventory deduction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall calculate the ingredient quantities to be deducted in each IQA document by multiplying the quantity of each product sold by the ingredient amounts specified in its formula, recursively resolving semi-product sub-formulas.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate deduction calculation requires full traversal of nested formula structures to reach raw ingredient level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall check current inventory levels for each ingredient before finalising an IQA document and apply substitution rules where stock is insufficient.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inventory-aware substitution prevents the IQA from referencing ingredients that are not physically available.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorised staff to manually review, edit, and approve a generated IQA document before it is posted to the inventory ledger.
generated_requirements.rationale: A manual review step ensures human oversight and compliance with audit-grade accounting standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall post approved IQA documents to the inventory ledger, updating the on-hand quantity of each affected ingredient accordingly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Posting the IQA is the final step that keeps inventory records current and accurate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent modification or deletion of a posted IQA document; corrections shall only be made via a new reversing or adjusting document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Immutability of posted documents is required to maintain an audit-compliant accounting trail.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow POS operators to build and maintain hierarchical product menus, supporting at least three levels of category nesting (e.g., category → sub-category → item).
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical menus are needed to organise diverse product offerings in a way that maps to POS terminal navigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow staff to add, remove, reorder, and rename menu items and categories within the hierarchical product menu.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full CRUD and ordering control over menu structure is necessary for day-to-day menu management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall associate each menu item with exactly one product formula, ensuring that sales of that item trigger the correct ingredient deductions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Linking menu items to formulas is the integration point between the POS sales data and inventory calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise the current approved product menu to connected POS terminals upon request or on a scheduled basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: POS terminals must reflect the latest menu to ensure sales data is captured against correct products.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall import daily sales transaction data from POS terminals, recording the product sold, quantity, date, and time for each transaction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sales data import is the trigger input for the automated IQA generation process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a master ingredient catalogue, allowing inventory managers to create, update, and deactivate ingredient records including name, unit of measure, and current stock level.
generated_requirements.rationale: A centralised ingredient catalogue is the foundation for formula definition and inventory tracking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support manual inventory adjustment entries independent of IQA documents, allowing staff to record stock receipts, wastage, and physical count corrections.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual adjustments are needed to handle inventory events not driven by sales, such as deliveries and spoilage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles: Inventory Manager, Formula Administrator, and POS Operator, each with a defined set of permitted actions.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC ensures that staff can only perform actions appropriate to their job function, reducing risk of errors and fraud.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require each user to authenticate with a unique username and password before accessing any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: Individual authentication is a prerequisite for enforcing RBAC and maintaining a meaningful audit trail.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a timestamped audit log entry for every create, update, delete, and post action performed on formulas, menus, inventory records, and IQA documents, capturing the user ID and before/after values.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit log is required to meet audit-compliant accounting standards and support forensic review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a reporting interface that allows authorised users to query and export IQA history, ingredient usage summaries, and current stock levels for a user-specified date range.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting capabilities are needed for management oversight, stock planning, and compliance verification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the relevant inventory manager via an in-application alert when any ingredient's on-hand quantity falls below a configurable reorder threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low-stock alerts enable proactive procurement and prevent service disruptions due to ingredient shortages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an administrator to create, deactivate, and assign roles to user accounts without requiring system downtime.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dynamic user management is necessary to accommodate staff changes without interrupting operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate that a product formula does not contain circular references (e.g., a semi-product that directly or indirectly references itself) and reject any formula that does.
generated_requirements.rationale: Circular formula references would cause infinite loops during IQA calculation and must be prevented at data entry time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support versioning of product formulas, retaining historical formula versions so that IQA documents can be recalculated or audited against the formula in effect on the date of sale.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula versioning is essential for accurate historical auditing when recipes change over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the automated generation of a daily IQA document, including all formula lookups and substitution evaluations, within 60 seconds for a sales volume of up to 10,000 line items per day.
generated_requirements.rationale: A 60-second processing ceiling ensures the IQA is available for review at the start of the next business period without delay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any interactive user action (e.g., saving a formula, loading a menu) within 3 seconds under normal operating load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sub-3-second response times maintain staff productivity and prevent frustration during routine operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at least 20 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response times beyond the stated performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple concurrent users are explicitly required by the use case and must not impact system responsiveness.

generated_requirements.text: All passwords shall be stored using a salted cryptographic hash (minimum bcrypt with cost factor 12 or equivalent) and shall never be stored or transmitted in plaintext.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure password storage protects user credentials in the event of a database breach.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the client application, the server, and POS terminals shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit prevents interception of sensitive business and inventory data on the local network.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock a user session after 15 minutes of inactivity, requiring re-authentication to resume.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorised access from unattended workstations in a restaurant environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account for a minimum of 30 minutes after five consecutive failed login attempts and notify the system administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout mitigates brute-force credential attacks against staff accounts.

generated_requirements.text: The audit log shall be append-only; no user, including administrators, shall be able to modify or delete audit log entries through the application interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: An immutable audit log is a fundamental requirement for audit-compliant accounting and fraud prevention.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall follow a consistent visual design language, with all primary workflows completable within no more than five navigation steps from the main dashboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Limiting navigation depth reduces training time and operational errors for restaurant staff who may not be technically proficient.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, human-readable validation error messages that identify the specific field and reason for rejection whenever user input fails validation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Descriptive error messages allow staff to self-correct data entry mistakes without requiring IT support.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide context-sensitive help text or tooltips for all major input fields and workflow steps.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline help reduces the learning curve for new staff and minimises reliance on external documentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an application uptime of at least 99.5% during defined business hours (06:00–24:00 daily), excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during business hours is critical to ensure POS integration and daily IQA generation are not disrupted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated database backups at least once every 24 hours and support point-in-time recovery to within 1 hour of any failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups and point-in-time recovery protect against data loss from hardware failure or corruption.

generated_requirements.text: In the event of an unexpected application crash or power failure, the system shall recover to a consistent state upon restart without requiring manual database repair.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic crash recovery ensures business continuity and prevents data corruption in an uncontrolled shutdown scenario.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run on the Windows operating system (Windows 10 or Windows Server 2016 and later versions) and shall not require hardware upgrades beyond the restaurant's existing infrastructure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Compatibility with existing Windows hardware and OS versions is an explicit constraint stated in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code shall adhere to a documented coding standard, and all modules shall include inline documentation sufficient for a developer unfamiliar with the codebase to understand their purpose within two hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Good code documentation reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements and bug fixes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected with a clear separation between the data access layer, business logic layer, and presentation layer, such that changes to one layer do not require changes to the others.
generated_requirements.rationale: Layered architecture reduces coupling and makes the system easier to modify, test, and extend over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include an automated regression test suite covering at least 80% of business logic code paths, executable as part of a continuous integration pipeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated tests reduce the risk of regressions when formulas, substitution logic, or IQA calculations are modified.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all unhandled exceptions and application errors to a persistent error log, including timestamp, user context, and stack trace, without exposing technical details to the end user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Detailed error logging accelerates diagnosis and resolution of production issues while keeping the UI user-friendly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce optimistic concurrency control on all shared records (formulas, inventory levels, IQA documents) to prevent lost updates when multiple users edit the same record simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrency control is essential in a multi-user environment to maintain data integrity without excessive locking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a system administrator to configure scheduled maintenance windows during which the application is gracefully suspended and users are notified in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scheduled maintenance windows allow database backups and updates to be performed with minimal operational disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support export of IQA documents and inventory reports in at least PDF and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable formats allow reports to be shared with external auditors and integrated with other business tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow formula administrators to set an effective date range for each formula version, ensuring that the correct version is automatically applied during IQA generation based on the sale date.
generated_requirements.rationale: Date-bound formula versions ensure historical accuracy when recipes change mid-period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard view for inventory managers displaying current stock levels, pending IQA documents awaiting approval, and ingredients below reorder threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: A consolidated dashboard gives inventory managers immediate situational awareness without navigating multiple screens.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that all monetary and quantity calculations within IQA documents are performed with sufficient numeric precision (minimum 4 decimal places) to prevent rounding errors from accumulating across large transaction volumes.
generated_requirements.rationale: High numeric precision is required to maintain audit-compliant accounting accuracy across high-volume daily sales data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow new customers to register an account by providing their full name, email address, password, and country of residence.
generated_requirements.rationale: User registration is the entry point for all customer interactions on the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to choose between a pay-as-you-go plan and an unlimited subscription plan during or after registration.
generated_requirements.rationale: The platform supports two distinct pricing models that must be selectable by the customer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate registered customers using their email address and password before granting access to protected features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication ensures that only registered users can access purchased content and account features.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to browse the movie catalog, organized by categories such as genre, country, language, and release year.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browsing by category enables customers to discover content relevant to their preferences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a search function that allows customers to find movies by title, actor, director, genre, or keyword.
generated_requirements.rationale: Search functionality is essential for customers to quickly locate specific films in a large catalog.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a movie detail page for each film, including title, synopsis, cast, director, duration, genre, language, release year, rating, and customer reviews.
generated_requirements.rationale: Detailed movie information helps customers make informed purchase decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow pay-as-you-go customers to purchase individual movies using a credit card or a pre-paid card.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pay-as-you-go customers require per-title purchase capability with multiple payment options.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow unlimited subscription customers to pay their recurring subscription fee via credit card or pre-paid card.
generated_requirements.rationale: Subscription billing must support the same payment methods available to pay-as-you-go customers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate pre-paid card codes against the database of activated cards before applying the card's balance to a transaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall grant a customer a two-day (48-hour) viewing window for each purchased movie, starting from the time of first playback.
generated_requirements.rationale: The two-day viewing window is a core business rule for pay-as-you-go content access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall stream purchased movies directly in the customer's web browser without requiring any additional software installation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow unlimited subscription customers to stream any available movie in the catalog for the duration of their active subscription.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to submit a written review and a star rating (1–5) for any movie they have purchased or streamed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display approved customer reviews on the corresponding movie detail page, showing the reviewer's display name, rating, review text, and submission date.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to view and update their account profile, including name, email address, password, and payment preferences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide customers with a purchase history page listing all movies bought or streamed, along with transaction dates, amounts paid, and viewing window status.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to cancel their unlimited subscription, with access remaining active until the end of the current billing period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to add new movies to the catalog, including uploading video files and entering all associated metadata.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to edit or remove existing movies from the catalog, including updating metadata and replacing video files.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to create, rename, and delete movie categories.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to view, suspend, reactivate, and delete customer accounts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to review, approve, or reject customer-submitted movie reviews before they are published on the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with a sales analytics dashboard displaying total revenue, revenue by movie, revenue by subscription plan, and revenue over configurable time periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with usage analytics including total streams, most-watched movies, active subscribers, and new registrations over configurable time periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send a confirmation email to a customer upon successful registration, purchase, subscription activation, and subscription cancellation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support adaptive bitrate streaming, automatically adjusting video quality based on the customer's available internet bandwidth.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, distinguishing between Customer, Administrator, and Manager roles with distinct permissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to reset their password via a time-limited, single-use link sent to their registered email address.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall facilitate asynchronous message exchange between the Customer Service Request (CSR) system and the Enterprise Asset Management (EAM/Datastream) system without requiring direct point-to-point interfaces between them.
generated_requirements.rationale: This is the core integration purpose of the platform — decoupling disparate systems via asynchronous messaging.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support real-time message routing, ensuring that messages published by a source system are delivered to the appropriate target system based on configurable routing rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dynamic routing is essential to correctly direct messages between multiple enterprise systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a message queuing mechanism that stores messages persistently until they are successfully consumed by the target system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persistent queuing ensures no message is lost if the target system is temporarily unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-based dashboard that allows System Administrators and Database Administrators to monitor real-time message traffic, including message volumes, throughput rates, and queue depths.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility through dashboards enables administrators to proactively manage platform health.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide transaction error tracking functionality that captures, logs, and displays failed or errored messages, including error codes, timestamps, source system, and target system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Error tracking is required so administrators can identify and diagnose integration failures quickly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow System Administrators to manually retry, reroute, or discard failed messages from the error tracking interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual intervention capabilities are necessary to resolve transaction errors without requiring system restarts or code changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide ad-hoc reporting capabilities that allow administrators to query message traffic data using configurable filters such as date range, source system, target system, message type, and status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ad-hoc reporting enables administrators to perform flexible analysis of historical and current message data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on the administrative dashboard, including average message processing time, error rate, message throughput, and queue wait time.
generated_requirements.rationale: KPIs provide quantifiable metrics that allow administrators to assess and report on system performance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all processed, failed, and retried messages in a persistent message archive for audit and transaction tracking purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Message retention is required to support audit trails and post-incident transaction reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC), distinguishing at minimum between System Administrator and Database Administrator roles with appropriate permissions for each.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC ensures that users can only perform actions appropriate to their designated role, reducing risk of unauthorized changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require authentication for all users accessing the administrative interface, supporting enterprise identity provider integration (e.g., LDAP or Active Directory).
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication via enterprise identity providers ensures consistent and centrally managed access control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform message integrity checking on all inbound and outbound messages to detect corruption or tampering during transmission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrity checking ensures that messages have not been altered in transit, maintaining data trustworthiness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support dynamic scaling, allowing additional processing nodes or resources to be added to the platform without service interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dynamic scaling is explicitly required to accommodate growth and changing workloads without downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support message transformation, converting messages from the format produced by the source system into the format expected by the target system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Format transformation is necessary to bridge schema and protocol differences between the CSR and EAM systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide configurable dead-letter queue (DLQ) handling, automatically moving messages that exceed a configurable retry threshold to a designated dead-letter queue for administrator review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dead-letter queues prevent poison messages from blocking processing while ensuring no message is silently discarded.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate automated alerts and notifications to System Administrators when error rates, queue depths, or processing latencies exceed configurable thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting enables administrators to respond to emerging issues before they impact business operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit log of all administrative actions performed within the platform, including login events, configuration changes, and manual message interventions.
generated_requirements.rationale: An immutable audit log supports accountability, compliance, and forensic investigation of administrative activity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configurable message time-to-live (TTL) settings per message type or queue, automatically expiring messages that have not been consumed within the defined period.
generated_requirements.rationale: TTL configuration prevents stale messages from being processed and consuming unnecessary resources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an interface for Database Administrators to execute and export ad-hoc queries against the message store and transaction logs.
generated_requirements.rationale: DBAs require direct query access to perform deep-dive analysis and support reporting needs beyond standard dashboards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at-least-once message delivery semantics, ensuring that every message is delivered to its target system even in the event of transient failures.
generated_requirements.rationale: At-least-once delivery guarantees that no message is lost due to infrastructure or network failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and deliver messages within a maximum end-to-end latency of 5 seconds under normal operating load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict time thresholds for message processing are explicitly required to support real-time integration between enterprise systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall sustain a throughput of at least 500 messages per second across all active queues without degradation in processing latency.
generated_requirements.rationale: High transaction volume handling is a core requirement, necessitating a defined minimum throughput benchmark.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 99.9% uptime availability during defined regular business hours, with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside of those hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-continuous availability during business operations is explicitly required to support uninterrupted city services.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned failure and resume normal message processing within a maximum Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined RTO ensures that failures are resolved quickly enough to minimize impact on dependent city systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all messages in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher between all integrated systems and the messaging platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive city data from interception during message exchange.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all messages and audit logs at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent approved encryption standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest ensures that stored messages and logs are protected against unauthorized access to underlying storage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authorization checks on every administrative API endpoint and UI action, rejecting requests from users who lack the required role permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent authorization enforcement prevents privilege escalation and unauthorized administrative operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock user accounts after five consecutive failed authentication attempts and require administrator intervention or a time-based unlock to restore access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies mitigate brute-force credential attacks on the administrative interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain archived messages and audit logs for a minimum of seven years to satisfy municipal records retention and audit requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term retention ensures compliance with government records management policies and supports historical audits.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall have a message loss rate of no greater than 0.001% under any operating condition, including partial infrastructure failures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Extremely low message loss is critical to maintaining data integrity across integrated city enterprise systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support horizontal scaling by adding processing nodes transparently, without requiring configuration changes to connected source or target systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent resource addition is explicitly required to accommodate growth without disrupting integrated systems.

generated_requirements.text: The administrative dashboard shall be accessible and fully functional on current versions of major web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) without requiring browser plugins.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-browser compatibility ensures administrators can access the platform from any standard city-issued workstation.

generated_requirements.text: The administrative user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to ensure usability for administrators with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance is required for government systems to meet federal and municipal accessibility mandates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present error messages and status notifications in plain, actionable language that allows administrators to understand the issue and take corrective action without consulting external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear, actionable error messaging reduces mean time to resolution for transaction errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on standard enterprise virtualization or container orchestration platforms (e.g., VMware, Kubernetes) without requiring proprietary hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: Platform-agnostic deployment reduces vendor lock-in and aligns with the City of Chicago's infrastructure standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase and configuration shall be structured to allow a developer with standard enterprise integration experience to understand, modify, and extend the platform within a reasonable onboarding period.
generated_requirements.rationale: Maintainability is essential for a long-lived government platform that will require ongoing updates and enhancements.

generated_requirements.text: All integration adapters and connectors for source and target systems shall be implemented as independently deployable, loosely coupled modules to allow updates without redeploying the entire platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular adapter design reduces the risk and effort of updating individual system integrations over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide comprehensive operational documentation, including runbooks for common administrative tasks, error resolution procedures, and scaling operations.

generated_requirements.rationale: Clustered broker redundancy ensures continued message processing if an individual node fails.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated health checks on all message queues, processing nodes, and integration adapters at intervals no greater than 60 seconds, and surface health status on the administrative dashboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Frequent automated health checks enable rapid detection of component failures before they escalate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete dashboard page loads and KPI refreshes within 3 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive dashboards are necessary for administrators to monitor high-volume message traffic in near real time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a configurable message prioritization scheme, allowing high-priority messages to be processed ahead of lower-priority messages within the same queue.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a searchable, paginated message trace view that allows administrators to look up the full lifecycle of any individual message by message ID, including all processing steps, timestamps, and status changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support scheduled and on-demand export of reports and message archive data in standard formats including CSV and PDF.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all inbound messages against a defined schema or contract before routing them, rejecting and logging malformed messages without forwarding them to target systems.

generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic retry with back-off reduces manual intervention for transient failures while avoiding retry storms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all inbound and outbound message metadata — including message ID, source, destination, size, timestamp, and processing duration — to a centralized, queryable transaction log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralized metadata logging is the foundation for monitoring, reporting, and audit capabilities across the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with external monitoring and observability tools (e.g., Splunk, Prometheus, or equivalent) via standard APIs or log forwarding protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration with enterprise monitoring tools allows the City of Chicago to incorporate platform telemetry into its broader IT operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo security vulnerability scanning and penetration testing at least annually, and all critical or high-severity findings shall be remediated within 30 days of discovery.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular security testing and timely remediation are required to maintain the platform's security posture over its operational lifetime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 5 minutes, ensuring that no more than 5 minutes of message transaction data can be lost in the event of a catastrophic failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: A tight RPO ensures that the volume of unrecoverable transaction data after a failure is bounded and acceptable for city operations.
