generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully operational on IBM's internal intranet infrastructure without requiring installation of client-side software beyond a standard web browser.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be compatible with the current IBM-standard web browsers (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Edge) without requiring browser plugins or extensions.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall be structured and documented such that a developer unfamiliar with the system can understand and modify a report module within one business day.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use a modular architecture that allows new report types to be added without modifying existing report modules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, human-readable error messages when a user action fails, describing the issue and suggesting a corrective action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent unauthorized users from accessing any grants data or report functionality and shall return an appropriate access-denied response for unauthorized requests.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to print reports directly from the browser with a print-optimized layout that omits navigation elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover automatically from a failed weekly data refresh by retrying the refresh process up to three times before alerting administrators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to select which columns to include or exclude in a generated report before viewing or exporting it.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard or home page that presents a high-level summary of key grants metrics upon user login.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not expose raw database connection strings, credentials, or internal system paths in any client-facing page source or error messages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include an online help section or user guide accessible from within the application that describes all major features and report types.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow team owners to create an account and log in using a unique username and password.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support role-based access control, distinguishing between at least two roles: Commissioner and Team Owner.
generated_requirements.rationale: Elevated commissioner privileges must be enforced separately from standard team owner capabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow team owners to add available NFL players to their roster.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adding players is a core roster management action required for team owners to compete in the league.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow team owners to drop players from their roster, returning those players to the available player pool.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dropping players is a fundamental roster management operation that keeps the player pool dynamic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow team owners to propose trades of one or more players with another team owner, who must accept or reject the trade.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trading is a key engagement mechanic in fantasy football that requires a two-party acceptance workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display up-to-date NFL player statistics, including but not limited to passing yards, rushing yards, receiving yards, touchdowns, and fantasy points earned.
generated_requirements.rationale: Current player statistics are essential for team owners to make informed roster decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall calculate and display fantasy points for each player based on the league's configured scoring settings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fantasy point calculation is the primary scoring mechanism that determines team standings and matchup outcomes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the commissioner to configure league-wide settings, including scoring rules, roster size limits, trade deadlines, and waiver wire rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioners must be able to govern the league by customizing rules to fit their league's preferences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the commissioner to adjust league settings at any point during the season, with changes taking effect as configured.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mid-season adjustments may be necessary to correct errors or respond to unforeseen circumstances.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a message board on the league page where participants can create, view, and reply to posts.
generated_requirements.rationale: A message board fosters community engagement and communication among league participants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the most recent message board posts prominently on the league home page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Surfacing recent posts keeps participants informed of active discussions without requiring navigation to the full board.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide navigation that allows users to move between the league page and individual team pages in no more than two clicks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Intuitive navigation between key pages reduces friction and improves the overall user experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a league standings page showing all teams ranked by wins, losses, and total fantasy points.
generated_requirements.rationale: League standings are a fundamental feature that allows participants to track competitive progress.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display each team's current roster, including player name, position, NFL team, and current fantasy point total.
generated_requirements.rationale: Team owners need a clear view of their roster composition to manage their team effectively.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow team owners to set a weekly starting lineup by selecting active players from their roster before the weekly scoring deadline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Setting a starting lineup is a core weekly action that directly affects a team's score for that matchup.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock individual player slots in a starting lineup once the player's NFL game has begun, preventing further lineup changes for that player.
generated_requirements.rationale: Locking active players prevents unfair retroactive lineup changes after real-world game outcomes are known.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate weekly head-to-head matchups between teams and display the live score for each matchup during the NFL game week.
generated_requirements.rationale: Head-to-head matchups are the primary competitive format and live scores drive real-time engagement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a searchable and filterable player pool showing all available (unowned) NFL players with their statistics and projected points.
generated_requirements.rationale: A searchable player pool enables team owners to efficiently identify and acquire the best available players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify team owners via email or in-app notification when a trade is proposed to them, accepted, or rejected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications ensure team owners can respond to trade proposals within any configured deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the commissioner to approve or veto completed trades before they are executed, if league settings require commissioner review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioner trade review is a common governance feature that protects league integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a transaction history log for each team, recording all adds, drops, and trades with timestamps.
generated_requirements.rationale: A transaction history provides transparency and accountability for all roster moves within the league.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the commissioner to invite new members to the league via email invitation containing a unique registration link.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email invitations provide a controlled and secure mechanism for the commissioner to populate the league.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce roster size limits as configured by the commissioner, preventing team owners from exceeding the maximum number of players.
generated_requirements.rationale: Roster limits are a fundamental league rule that must be enforced programmatically to ensure fairness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display player injury status (e.g., Active, Questionable, Out, IR) alongside player information throughout the platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Injury status is critical information that directly influences roster and lineup decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow team owners to reset their password via a secure email-based password reset flow.
generated_requirements.rationale: Password recovery is a standard account management requirement to prevent users from being permanently locked out.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of 99.9% or greater, measured on a monthly basis, throughout the NFL season (September through February).
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is explicitly required to reliably support all user transactions throughout the NFL season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return page load responses within 2 seconds for 95% of requests under normal operating load.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at least 10,000 concurrent users without degradation of response time beyond the defined performance thresholds.

generated_requirements.text: All user passwords shall be stored using a strong, salted cryptographic hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2).

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authorization checks on every API endpoint to ensure users can only access or modify resources they are permitted to.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement protection against common web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and cross-site request forgery (CSRF).

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage and restore full service within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 30 minutes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated database backups at least once every 24 hours, with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 1 hour.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a user-friendly error message when an operation fails, without exposing internal system details or stack traces to the end user.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete all roster transaction operations (add, drop, trade execution) and persist the result within 3 seconds of user submission.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to handle increased load during peak NFL game-day traffic without requiring manual intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an intranet-based web application accessible to finance staff and managers across PCG's branch, complex, district, and regional organizational levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control so that each user can only view and interact with functionality and data appropriate to their role within the PCG organizational hierarchy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access prevents unauthorized viewing or modification of budget data outside a user's scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support distinct user roles corresponding to each organizational level: branch, complex, district, and regional.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply the current year's approved formulae to drive all revenue and expense calculations within budget submissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent formula application ensures all budget calculations are standardized and auditable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow finance staff to create, edit, and save annual budget submissions for their assigned organizational unit.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to submit completed budget submissions for review and approval by the appropriate manager at the next level of the PCG hierarchy.

generated_requirements.rationale: Approval workflow actions enable managers to control the quality and accuracy of budget submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to enter, modify, and delete individual revenue line items within their budget submission.

generated_requirements.rationale: Fast recovery with data integrity protection minimizes the impact of outages on the budgeting process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data transmitted between the client browser and the server using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encrypting data in transit protects sensitive financial information from interception on the intranet.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt sensitive financial data at rest in the database using an industry-standard encryption algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects confidential budget data in the event of unauthorized database access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate a user session after 15 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication before access is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access from unattended workstations on the intranet.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication events, access control decisions, and data modification actions in a tamper-evident security log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Security logging supports forensic investigation and compliance with financial data governance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present all user interface elements, labels, and messages in plain, unambiguous language appropriate for finance staff without requiring technical knowledge.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear language reduces user errors and the need for training, improving adoption across all organizational levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide context-sensitive help or tooltips for all formula-driven fields and complex input areas within the budget submission form.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline help reduces user confusion about how calculated fields work and what inputs are expected.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a new finance staff user to complete their first budget submission with no more than 30 minutes of self-directed orientation using built-in guidance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of onboarding is important given the annual nature of the task and potential staff turnover between cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed using a modular, layered architecture that allows individual components (e.g., formula engine, BDW integration, UAS integration) to be updated independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of annual updates to formulae, integrations, and business rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include comprehensive technical documentation covering architecture, data models, integration interfaces, and deployment procedures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Complete documentation enables future developers and administrators to maintain and extend the system effectively.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the annual formula update to be completed and tested by an administrator without requiring code changes or application redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configuration-driven formula management reduces the operational burden and risk associated with the annual update process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle BDW integration failures gracefully, displaying a clear error message to the user and logging the failure, without corrupting existing budget data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation protects data integrity and user experience when the BDW is temporarily unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle UAS integration failures gracefully, preventing login but displaying an informative message, and shall not allow unauthenticated access under any failure condition.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fail-secure behavior during UAS outages ensures that authentication cannot be bypassed due to integration errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform a full backup of all budget submission data at least once every 24 hours, with backups stored in a location separate from the primary database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups ensure that budget data can be recovered in the event of hardware failure or data corruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on PCG's existing intranet server infrastructure without requiring the procurement of additional specialized hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: Infrastructure compatibility reduces deployment costs and simplifies the rollout of the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain historical budget submission data for a minimum of seven years to support financial auditing and trend analysis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term data retention supports regulatory compliance and enables year-over-year budget comparison.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to view and compare budget submissions from prior years alongside the current year's submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Historical comparison helps users identify trends and produce more accurate forecasts for the current cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative interface for monitoring system health, active user sessions, BDW synchronization status, and integration connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: An admin monitoring interface enables proactive identification and resolution of operational issues during the budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale to accommodate a growth of up to 50% in the number of users and organizational units without requiring architectural changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability headroom ensures the system remains viable as PCG's organizational structure grows over time.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be available 24/7 especially during the budgeting period. The RFS system shall be available 90% of the time all year and 98% during the budgeting period. 2% of the time the system will become available within 1 hour of the time that the situation is reported.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum hardware requirements prior to implementation. Minimum Hard Disk Space: 400MB.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum software requirements prior to implementation: Microsoft Internet Explorer 5.0. Microsoft .NET Framework 1.1. Microsoft Internet Explorer Web Controls
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be able to easily interface with the BDW environment in order to load the data. Seven data files shall be received from the BDW and loaded into the RFS system within 3 hours one day prior to the start of budgeting process every year. The data will be as of October month-end. Test files (7) shall be loaded up to 5 times throughout the year from the BDW.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Changes to revenue and expense formulae must be applied to the RFS system every year prior to the budgeting process start. Revenue and expense formulae will be updated within one week of their receipt after which 2 weeks will be allotted to testing of the budget calculations to be conducted jointly by the project development team and PCG Finance.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: New branches/complexes/districts/regions and revenue/expense accounts added to the BDW must be reflected in the RFS system. 100% of the entities (mentioned above) available as of October month-end will be loaded into the RFS system prior to the budget start process.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only authorized user shall access the RFS system. ONLY users that exist in the UAS system with the RFS system assigned to their role(s) shall access the RFS system and use it.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: User access should be limited to the permissions granted to their role(s) Each level in the PCG hierarchy will be assigned a role and users will be assigned to these roles. Access to functionality within RFS system is dependent on the privileges/permission assigned to the role.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must comply with the intranet page standards and requirements of ENET Securities. 95% of the product look & feel will be approved by the Architecture group prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected and approved within one month of product release.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The intranet pages should display appropriately in all resolutions from 800x600 and higher. The display of all the pages will be correct in 95% of the resolutions prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected within a week of notification of the incorrect display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The users should be able to easily use the system to successfully complete their budgets within the stipulated time for completion. With a week of training prior to product implementation 98% of the users shall complete their budgets within the stipulated time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product interface should be fast. The response time of the product interface should not exceed 2 seconds 90% of the time. For 10% of the time the response time should not be more than 10 seconds.not be more than 10 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR
